decisions.decision: Thin bootstrap script (remote-install.sh) that clones repo to temp dir, runs install.sh, cleans up
decisions.rationale: Single file to host/curl. Delegates all logic to install.sh. No duplication.

decisions.decision: Support both private (git clone via SSH/gh) and public (curl from raw.githubusercontent.com) access
decisions.rationale: Repo is private now but will be public. Script auto-detects availability.

decisions.decision: remote-install.sh accepts: target dir (positional), --ref <tag/branch>, --update, --uninstall, --force (passthrough to install.sh)
decisions.rationale: Supports version pinning and all lifecycle operations via passthrough.

decisions.decision: Clone to mktemp -d, trap cleanup on EXIT to ensure temp dir is always removed
decisions.rationale: No leftover state on the user's machine after install.

decisions.decision: remote-install.sh lives at repo root alongside install.sh
Batch-fix all pre-existing validation errors and warnings before adding new artifacts
decisions.rationale: Clean baseline prevents noise in future reviews. 14 REVIEW.json schemaVersion errors and assorted warnings accumulated across old features.

constraints: Bash only — no Python/Node dependencies in the bootstrap script
Must work on macOS and Linux
Must handle git clone failure gracefully (private repo without access)
install.sh is the single source of truth for installation logic — remote-install.sh is only a wrapper
Temp dir must be cleaned up even on failure (trap EXIT)
Validation cleanup must not change semantics of old artifacts — only fix schema compliance and path ambiguity
